channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How Trump may be changing his stance on AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration's approach on AI so far has been to prioritize innovation over regulation. But recently, there's been a shift in rhetoric.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration's approach on AI so far has been to prioritize innovation over regulation. But recently, there's been a shift in rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman takes the stand to fend off Elon Musk's accusations he 'stole a charity'
item.description: The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.
item.content:encoded:
The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.

(Image credit: JOSH EDELSON)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
item.description: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.content:encoded: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The clipping economy: How short-form video 'clippers' are overrunning the internet
item.description: Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.
item.content:encoded:
Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.

(Image credit: Emrah Bayraktar)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Several states considering ban on legal personhood for AI
item.description: Is it time to consider prosecuting AI models for breaking the law? Some say it's time. Others say that would be an insult to humanity. Some states are considering laws blocking AI personhood.
item.content:encoded: Is it time to consider prosecuting AI models for breaking the law? Some say it's time. Others say that would be an insult to humanity. Some states are considering laws blocking AI personhood.
item.dc:creator: Martin Kaste

item.title: Canvas is back online, but questions — and final exam disruptions — linger
item.description: Some schools are warning users not to log back into Canvas yet, after a ransomware group claimed credit for a data breach. Half of North America's higher education institutions use the platform.
item.content:encoded:
Some schools are warning users not to log back into Canvas yet, after a ransomware group claimed credit for a data breach. Half of North America's higher education institutions use the platform.

(Image credit: Michael Warren)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: How Silicon Valley's new tech right has profited by aligning with MAGA
item.description: Atlantic writer George Packer discusses how tech venture capitalists, who are heavily invested in AI and cryptocurrency, aligned with Trump and influenced policies related to their own investments.
item.content:encoded: Atlantic writer George Packer discusses how tech venture capitalists, who are heavily invested in AI and cryptocurrency, aligned with Trump and influenced policies related to their own investments.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over claims chatbot posed as doctor
item.description: State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.
item.content:encoded:
State officials allege a Character.AI bot claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist and provided a fake state medical license number.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
item.description: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.
item.content:encoded:
Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

(Image credit: Thos Robinson)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
item.description: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.
item.content:encoded:
The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

(Image credit: Eliana Morales Gil)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn
